steps.title: Spot the key signal
steps.content: The passage says grouping authors into literary movements "encourages literary scholars to ______ subtleties." The next sentence shows what this looks like: scholars studying Channing "may inadvertently overlook nuances" by focusing only on the most obvious traits. The blank must mean to overlook or treat as unimportant.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice
steps.content:
The word needs to mean to overlook or dismiss as unimportant. Substitute each choice:
  • Choice A — discount: means to treat as unimportant or disregard — directly parallels "overlook nuances" from the following sentence. ✓
  • Choice B — scrutinize: means to examine closely — the opposite of what the passage describes; grouping authors into movements leads scholars away from close attention to subtleties, not toward it. ✗
  • Choice C — magnify: means to enlarge or make seem greater — scholars ignoring subtleties are not amplifying them; this is the wrong direction. ✗
  • Choice D — denounce: means to publicly criticize or condemn — scholars are overlooking subtleties, not criticizing them; this introduces a hostile connotation absent from the passage. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
